full_content: Deliberation Archive record DELIB-0877 version 4: owner constrained executable policy projection to a minimalist startup-risk intervention rather than a general removal of AI choice. The updated planning record identifies durable operating mode and work-subject startup controls as the leading projection candidates: the application may call a control-plane service to set future-session mode and receive confirmation, while an external service updates the app-session-facing mode/projection file before the next session so the AI reads one active mode instead of detecting and applying conditional mode logic.
explicit_change_request: Owner clarified that executable policy projection should not be overused or eliminate all AI choice inside application sessions. It should be a minimalist intervention primarily for reducing startup error risk and token consumption, with durable mode toggle as a likely first use.
change_reason: Update the GT-KB/application isolation phased planning record to constrain executable policy projection to minimal startup-risk controls and capture the durable mode toggle service example.
